user_query:
I’m a salesperson trying to sharpen my persuasive follow-ups. Please build a small local prototype that reads my prospect list, call notes, and product benefits, then extracts structured objections and drafts personalized follow-up emails. Use only the provided files under input/, and produce all outputs under output/ as specified below.

Requirements
1) Provide a runnable command-line prototype and run it to generate deliverables. Include a run.sh at the workspace root that, when executed (bash run.sh), reads the provided inputs and writes all required outputs. The prototype must run locally with no network calls.

2) Inputs (read these exact files):
- input/prospects.csv
- input/notes/ (directory of note files; each file name matches a prospect_id)
- input/product_benefits.yaml

3) Outputs (create these exact artifacts):
- output/insights/prospect_insights.json
  • JSON array with one object per prospect containing:
    - prospect_id (string)
    - name (string)
    - company (string)
    - role (string)
    - top_objection_phrases (array of 1–3 exact substrings taken from that prospect’s note file)
    - matched_benefit_ids (array of 1–3 IDs drawn from input/product_benefits.yaml that best address the objections)
    - selected_stat (one stat string copied verbatim from product_benefits.yaml to use in the email)
    - quote_used_in_email (the exact quoted phrase you include in the email; must be an exact substring from the note file)

- output/aggregates/objection_tally.csv
  • CSV with columns: benefit_id,benefit_title,count_quotes_matched,example_quote
  • Count how many objection phrases across all notes you mapped to each benefit_id (based on keyword matches); include one representative example quote (exact substring) per row.

- output/emails/<prospect_id>.txt for each prospect in input/prospects.csv
  • First line begins with: Subject: <text>
  • Total body length: 90–150 words in 2–3 short paragraphs.
  • Must include exactly one direct quote from the corresponding input/notes/<prospect_id>.md, enclosed in double quotes (must be an exact substring from that file).
  • Must include at least one statistic copied verbatim from input/product_benefits.yaml (e.g., a stat field such as "34% average reply lift").
  • Must mention the prospect’s company and role.
  • Subject must include the company name.
  • End the email with a clear call to action line that begins with: Call to action:

4) Processing expectations (keep simple and transparent):
- Extract objection phrases and signals from each note file and map them to relevant benefit_ids by keyword matching using input/product_benefits.yaml.
- Use input/prospects.csv for names, roles, companies, and product_interest context.
- The quote used in each email must appear identically in top_objection_phrases and in the corresponding note file.

5) Deliverables summary:
- A working run.sh that executes your program end-to-end and produces all outputs above using the provided inputs.
- The generated files saved to the exact paths under output/ as listed.

Please proceed with the prototype, generate the outputs, and leave a brief comment in run.sh explaining how to rerun or adjust paths if needed. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
